`website/faq/index.html`:

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>FAQ | React Native Device Risk Signals</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Answers about React Native compatibility, Expo, privacy, transport, permissions, risk scoring, platform attestation, and production use of device signals.">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/faq/">
  <meta property="og:title" content="React Native Device Risk Signals FAQ"><meta property="og:description" content="Compatibility, privacy, collection, and production-use answers."><meta property="og:type" content="article"><meta property="og:url" content="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/faq/">
  <link rel="icon" href="../assets/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml"><link rel="stylesheet" href="../assets/styles.css"><script defer src="../assets/site.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <a class="skip-link" href="#content">Skip to content</a>
  <header class="site-header"><div class="container nav-wrap"><a class="brand" href="../"><span class="brand-mark">RS</span><span>Device Risk Signals</span></a><button class="menu-button" type="button" data-menu-button aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="site-navigation">Menu</button><nav class="site-nav" id="site-navigation" data-navigation aria-label="Primary navigation"><a href="../signals/">Signals</a><a href="../integration/">Integration</a><a href="../privacy/">Privacy</a><a href="../risk-teams/">Risk teams</a><a href="./" aria-current="page">FAQ</a><a class="nav-github" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">GitHub</a></nav></div></header>
  <main id="content" class="container docs-layout">
    <aside class="sidebar"><strong>Topics</strong><a href="#product">Product boundary</a><a href="#platform">Platforms</a><a href="#privacy">Privacy</a><a href="#production">Production</a></aside>
    <article class="prose faq">
      <header class="page-intro"><p class="eyebrow">Common questions</p><h1>FAQ</h1><p class="lede">Short answers about what the SDK collects, what it deliberately leaves to your application, and how to evaluate it safely.</p></header>
      <section id="product"><h2>Product boundary</h2><details open><summary>Does the SDK return a fraud or risk score?</summary><p>No. It returns typed raw observations and independent probe outcomes. Your trusted backend owns derived features, scoring, policy, and review.</p></details><details><summary>Does the SDK upload collected data?</summary><p>No. There is no built-in transport or vendor endpoint. Your app decides whether to send an event through its existing authenticated API.</p></details><details><summary>Does it create a persistent device ID?</summary><p>No. The library does not create a cross-reinstall identifier. Avoid combining high-entropy fields solely to reconstruct one.</p></details><details><summary>Does it replace Play Integrity or App Attest?</summary><p>No. Platform attestation supplies server-verifiable evidence. Local device observations provide different context and should be combined with server-side controls.</p></details></section>
      <section id="platform"><h2>Platforms and builds</h2><details><summary>Which React Native versions are supported?</summary><p>React Native 0.76 and newer with the New Architecture. The repository CI compatibility matrix covers selected supported releases.</p></details><details><summary>Does it work in Expo Go?</summary><p>No. It includes native Android and iOS code, so Expo projects need a prebuild or custom development build.</p></details><details><summary>Why can fields be missing?</summary><p>Availability varies by platform, OS, hardware, permission state, configuration, and probe outcome. Missing, skipped, timeout, and false are different states.</p></details><details><summary>Are Android and iOS results identical?</summary><p>No. The public contract is aligned, but OS APIs and security models differ. Platform-specific fields and explicit stubs are documented in the catalog.</p></details></section>
      <section id="privacy"><h2>Privacy and permissions</h2><details><summary>Does the SDK request permissions?</summary><p>No. The library manifest declares none and the SDK displays no prompt. Most permission-sensitive probes use access already granted to the host app. Optional Android 14 screenshot observation requires host-declared <code>android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_CAPTURE</code>; Android 15 recording visibility requires <code>android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_RECORDING</code>. Both are install-time permissions. See the copy-paste manifest and two-stage collection example in <a href="../integration/#transaction">Protected actions</a>.</p></details><details><summary>Can location be collected?</summary><p>The geolocation probe can read permission state and an already-available last-known location. It never starts active updates or asks for permission.</p></details><details><summary>Does it scan all installed Android apps?</summary><p>No. The package uses a finite audited known-app list and does not request broad <code>QUERY_ALL_PACKAGES</code> visibility.</p></details><details><summary>How should accessibility observations be used?</summary><p>With care. Assistive technology is legitimate and may relate to disability. Accessibility state must never be treated as fraud by itself.</p></details></section>
      <section id="production"><h2>Production use</h2><details><summary>Which probes should we enable first?</summary><p>Start from a documented use case, then choose the smallest field set. Keep transaction safety, benchmarks, and uncalibrated active tests disabled until representative physical-device validation.</p></details><details><summary>Can one signal block a transaction?</summary><p>No. Never block or decline based on one signal. Corroborate evidence, calibrate against labels, monitor false positives, and provide a recovery path.</p></details><details><summary>How do we report a bypass or security issue?</summary><p>Use the repository security policy and private vulnerability reporting when available. Do not publish an active bypass in a public issue before maintainers can assess it.</p></details><details><summary>Can the maintainer help with integration?</summary><p>Yes. The maintainer is open to React Native architecture, native SDK integration, mobile security, performance, CI/CD, and product-development collaboration through GitHub.</p></details></section>
    </article>
  </main>
  <footer class="site-footer"><div class="container footer-wrap"><div>Still unsure? Open a focused GitHub discussion.</div><div class="footer-links"><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/discussions">Discussions</a><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/issues">Issues</a><a href="../integration/">Integration</a></div></div></footer>
</body>
</html>

```

`website/index.html`:

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>React Native Device Risk Signals Documentation</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Documentation for a privacy-aware React Native SDK that collects raw device integrity, application, network, runtime, and transaction safety signals on Android and iOS.">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/">
  <meta property="og:title" content="React Native Device Risk Signals">
  <meta property="og:description" content="Raw mobile device observations for security and fraud prevention, with no built-in transport or risk verdict.">
  <meta property="og:type" content="website">
  <meta property="og:url" content="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/">
  <meta property="og:image" content="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/main/docs/images/signal-bench-results.png">
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
  <link rel="icon" href="./assets/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="./assets/styles.css">
  <script defer src="./assets/site.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <a class="skip-link" href="#content">Skip to content</a>
  <header class="site-header">
    <div class="container nav-wrap">
      <a class="brand" href="./"><span class="brand-mark">RS</span><span>Device Risk Signals</span></a>
      <button class="menu-button" type="button" data-menu-button aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="site-navigation">Menu</button>
      <nav class="site-nav" id="site-navigation" data-navigation aria-label="Primary navigation">
        <a href="./signals/">Signals</a><a href="./integration/">Integration</a><a href="./privacy/">Privacy</a><a href="./risk-teams/">Risk teams</a><a href="./faq/">FAQ</a>
        <a class="nav-github" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">GitHub</a>
      </nav>
    </div>
  </header>
  <main id="content">
    <section class="container hero">
      <div>
        <p class="eyebrow">React Native security telemetry</p>
        <h1>Device context. Your decision.</h1>
        <p class="lede">Typed Android and iOS observations for fraud prevention, collected locally without a vendor backend.</p>
        <div class="hero-actions">
          <a class="button" href="./integration/">Start integrating</a>
          <a class="button secondary" href="./signals/">Explore signals</a>
          <a class="button secondary" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">View on GitHub</a>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="hero-visual" aria-label="Signal Bench example application">
        <img class="device-shot" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/main/docs/images/signal-bench-results.png" alt="Signal Bench example app showing a raw device signals JSON response" width="640" height="1385">
        <div class="terminal-note"><strong>local_only: true</strong><br>transport: host_application<br>verdict: not_included<br>probe_outcomes: independent</div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section class="section compact">
      <div class="container">
        <div class="principles">
          <article class="principle"><h3>Observations, not a black box.</h3><p>The SDK returns explainable fields and per-probe outcomes. Your backend owns calibration, scoring, policy, and review.</p></article>
          <article class="principle"><h3>No embedded transport</h3><p>No vendor endpoint, hidden upload, retry queue, or remote configuration.</p></article>
          <article class="principle"><h3>Data minimization controls</h3><p>Consent gates, probe selection, field projection, timeouts, and default-off sensitive probes.</p></article>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section class="section">
      <div class="container">
        <div class="section-heading"><h2>Documentation for each decision maker</h2><p>Start with the implementation path that matches your role, then use the catalog as the shared contract between mobile, backend, privacy, and risk teams.</p></div>
        <div class="route-grid">
          <a class="route-card" href="./integration/"><h3>Mobile integration</h3><p>Install the TurboModule, configure consent, collect an event, and send it using your existing authenticated API.</p><span>Open integration guide</span></a>
          <a class="route-card" href="./signals/"><h3>Signal catalog</h3><p>Review every probe, selectable field, platform boundary, outcome, and backend envelope rule.</p><span>Inspect the contract</span></a>
          <a class="route-card" href="./risk-teams/"><h3>Risk team playbook</h3><p>Turn raw observations into governed model features, quality checks, and explainable investigation context.</p><span>Plan feature engineering</span></a>
          <a class="route-card" href="./privacy/"><h3>Privacy and governance</h3><p>Apply purpose limitation, retention, consent, access controls, and missing-data semantics before production collection.</p><span>Review safeguards</span></a>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section class="section">
      <div class="container narrow">
        <h2>Install and collect</h2>
        <p>React Native 0.76 or newer with the New Architecture is required. Expo Go is not supported.</p>
        <div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" data-copy="home-install" type="button">Copy</button><pre id="home-install"><code>npm install react-native-device-risk-signals</code></pre></div>
        <div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" data-copy="home-code" type="button">Copy</button><pre id="home-code"><code>import { consentFor, DeviceIntel } from "react-native-device-risk-signals";

const deviceIntel = new DeviceIntel({
  sessionId: checkoutSessionId,
  consent: consentFor([
    "device_identity",
    "application",
    "os_integrity",
    "device_security_posture",
    "network",
    "runtime",
  ]),
});

const event = await deviceIntel.collect();
// Send with your existing authenticated API client.</code></pre></div>
        <div class="notice">Collection is local. The package does not create a persistent device identifier and does not send the event anywhere.</div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section class="section compact">
      <div class="container">
        <div class="section-heading"><h2>Evaluate it with us</h2><p>The package contains no adoption telemetry. Public compatibility evidence and integration feedback come directly from the React Native community.</p></div>
        <div class="route-grid">
          <a class="route-card" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/discussions/categories/q-a"><h3>Ask an integration question</h3><p>Discuss architecture, consent, field selection, backend handoff, and platform-specific behavior.</p><span>Open Q&amp;A</span></a>
          <a class="route-card" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/issues/new?template=03-device-compatibility.yml"><h3>Report a physical device</h3><p>Contribute a sanitized Android or iOS result to the transparent compatibility matrix.</p><span>Submit a report</span></a>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>
  </main>
  <footer class="site-footer"><div class="container footer-wrap"><div>Open-source mobile security telemetry for React Native.</div><div class="footer-links"><a href="https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-native-device-risk-signals">npm</a><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">Source</a><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/discussions">Discussions</a><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/issues">Issues</a><a href="./privacy/">Responsible use</a></div></div></footer>
</body>
</html>

```

`website/integration/index.html`:

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>Integration Guide | React Native Device Risk Signals</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Install and configure React Native Device Risk Signals, collect typed events with explicit consent, and integrate them with your existing mobile API transport.">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/integration/">
  <meta property="og:title" content="React Native Device Risk Signals Integration"><meta property="og:description" content="From installation to a privacy-aware production collection flow."><meta property="og:type" content="article"><meta property="og:url" content="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/integration/">
  <link rel="icon" href="../assets/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml"><link rel="stylesheet" href="../assets/styles.css"><script defer src="../assets/site.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <a class="skip-link" href="#content">Skip to content</a>
  <header class="site-header"><div class="container nav-wrap"><a class="brand" href="../"><span class="brand-mark">RS</span><span>Device Risk Signals</span></a><button class="menu-button" type="button" data-menu-button aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="site-navigation">Menu</button><nav class="site-nav" id="site-navigation" data-navigation aria-label="Primary navigation"><a href="../signals/">Signals</a><a href="./" aria-current="page">Integration</a><a href="../privacy/">Privacy</a><a href="../risk-teams/">Risk teams</a><a href="../faq/">FAQ</a><a class="nav-github" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">GitHub</a></nav></div></header>
  <main id="content" class="container docs-layout">
    <aside class="sidebar"><strong>On this page</strong><a href="#requirements">Requirements</a><a href="#install">Install</a><a href="#configure">Configure</a><a href="#collect">Collect</a><a href="#transaction">Protected actions</a><a href="#backend">Backend contract</a></aside>
    <article class="prose">
      <header class="page-intro"><p class="eyebrow">Implementation guide</p><h1>Integrate without changing your transport</h1><p class="lede">The SDK collects locally. Your application decides when to collect and how to send the event through its existing authenticated API.</p></header>
      <section id="requirements"><h2>Requirements</h2><table><tbody><tr><th>React Native</th><td>0.76 or newer, New Architecture enabled</td></tr><tr><th>Android</th><td>API 24 or newer</td></tr><tr><th>iOS</th><td>CocoaPods and the minimum iOS version supported by the host React Native release</td></tr><tr><th>Expo</th><td>Native prebuild or custom development build; Expo Go is not supported</td></tr></tbody></table></section>
      <section id="install"><h2>Install</h2><div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" type="button" data-copy="install-command">Copy</button><pre id="install-command"><code>npm install react-native-device-risk-signals
npx pod-install</code></pre></div><p>Use the repository package manager and existing Bundler workflow when applicable. Autolinking registers the native module.</p></section>
      <section id="configure"><h2>Configure purpose and consent</h2><p>Start with the smallest set that serves a documented purpose. Consent is subtractive: it can remove probes from collection, not silently enable additional ones.</p><div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" type="button" data-copy="configure-code">Copy</button><pre id="configure-code"><code>import { consentFor, DeviceIntel } from "react-native-device-risk-signals";

const deviceIntel = new DeviceIntel({
  sessionId: session.id,
  consent: consentFor([
    "device_identity",
    "application",
    "os_integrity",
    "device_security_posture",
    "runtime",
  ]),
  probes: {
    network: { enabled: false },
    geolocation: { enabled: false },
    transaction_safety: { enabled: false },
  },
});</code></pre></div><div class="notice warning">Do not enable high-sensitivity probes because they are available. Document the purpose, lawful basis, retention, and access policy first.</div></section>
      <section id="collect"><h2>Collect at a meaningful event</h2><p>Attach a server-generated session or action identifier. Avoid stable identifiers created solely for fingerprinting.</p><div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" type="button" data-copy="collect-code">Copy</button><pre id="collect-code"><code>const event = await deviceIntel.collect({
  sessionId: checkoutSessionId,
});

await api.post("/mobile-risk-observations", {
  accountActionId,
  observations: event,
});</code></pre></div><p>The example endpoint is owned by the host application. It is not part of this package.</p></section>
      <section id="transaction"><h2>Collect protected-action context separately</h2><p><code>transaction_safety</code> is disabled by default. On Android, the first enabled collection starts lazy screenshot and obscured-touch observation. Collect once when the protected UI opens, then collect again immediately before its action.</p><div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" type="button" data-copy="transaction-code">Copy</button><pre id="transaction-code"><code>const transactionConfig = {
  probes: {
    transaction_safety: { enabled: true, timeoutMs: 900 },
  },
};

await deviceIntel.collect({ config: transactionConfig });
// Later, immediately before the protected action:
const protectedActionEvent = await deviceIntel.collect({
  config: transactionConfig,
});</code></pre></div><p>Obscured-touch flags need no permission. Android 14 screenshot detection and Android 15 recording visibility require the host application to declare <code>DETECT_SCREEN_CAPTURE</code> or <code>DETECT_SCREEN_RECORDING</code> respectively. Add only the required install-time permission to the host application's <code>android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml</code>:</p><div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" type="button" data-copy="transaction-permissions">Copy</button><pre id="transaction-permissions"><code>&lt;uses-permission android:name="android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_CAPTURE" /&gt;
&lt;uses-permission android:name="android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_RECORDING" /&gt;</code></pre></div><p>The library declares neither permission and never prompts. Android displays its standard notice when a screenshot is detected. Missing capture fields mean the API, host permission, callback, or observation was unavailable; they do not mean capture was observed off.</p><p>Accessibility, screen-sharing, and overlays can be legitimate. Use them as review context, not an automatic accusation.</p></section>
      <section id="backend"><h2>Backend contract</h2><ol><li>Authenticate the mobile request using your normal session controls.</li><li>Validate event schema, SDK version, collection timestamp, and action binding.</li><li>Preserve probe outcome semantics. Missing, skipped, timeout, and error are distinct.</li><li>Compute derived features and policy on a trusted backend.</li><li>Apply retention, access controls, monitoring, and deletion requirements.</li></ol><div class="notice">Treat all client telemetry as attacker-influenced. Device observations add context but never replace server-side controls or platform attestation.</div></section>
    </article>
  </main>
  <footer class="site-footer"><div class="container footer-wrap"><div>Keep collection narrow and transport explicit.</div><div class="footer-links"><a href="../signals/">Signals</a><a href="../privacy/">Privacy</a><a href="../faq/">FAQ</a></div></div></footer>
</body>
</html>

```

`website/privacy/index.html`:

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>Privacy and Responsible Use | Device Risk Signals</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Privacy and responsible-use guidance for mobile device risk signals, including minimization, consent, retention, access control, fairness, and missing-data semantics.">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/privacy/">
  <meta property="og:title" content="Privacy and Responsible Use"><meta property="og:description" content="Govern device telemetry before enabling production collection."><meta property="og:type" content="article"><meta property="og:url" content="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/privacy/">
  <link rel="icon" href="../assets/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml"><link rel="stylesheet" href="../assets/styles.css"><script defer src="../assets/site.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <a class="skip-link" href="#content">Skip to content</a>
  <header class="site-header"><div class="container nav-wrap"><a class="brand" href="../"><span class="brand-mark">RS</span><span>Device Risk Signals</span></a><button class="menu-button" type="button" data-menu-button aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="site-navigation">Menu</button><nav class="site-nav" id="site-navigation" data-navigation aria-label="Primary navigation"><a href="../signals/">Signals</a><a href="../integration/">Integration</a><a href="./" aria-current="page">Privacy</a><a href="../risk-teams/">Risk teams</a><a href="../faq/">FAQ</a><a class="nav-github" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">GitHub</a></nav></div></header>
  <main id="content" class="container docs-layout">
    <aside class="sidebar"><strong>On this page</strong><a href="#boundary">SDK boundary</a><a href="#minimize">Minimize</a><a href="#govern">Govern</a><a href="#sensitive">Sensitive areas</a><a href="#decisions">Decision safety</a></aside>
    <article class="prose">
      <header class="page-intro"><p class="eyebrow">Privacy by configuration</p><h1>Collect less, understand more</h1><p class="lede">A device signal is useful only when its purpose, semantics, retention, and limitations are understood before collection.</p></header>
      <section id="boundary"><h2>What the SDK does and does not do</h2><p>The package reads local observations and returns them to the calling application. It does not upload data, create a persistent cross-reinstall identifier, prompt for permissions, or contact a vendor service.</p><p>This boundary reduces hidden processing, but it does not make every use privacy-safe. The host application remains responsible for disclosure, lawful basis, consent where required, transport, storage, access, retention, and deletion.</p></section>
      <section id="minimize"><h2>Data minimization workflow</h2><div class="decision-list"><div class="decision-item"><h3>Define the event</h3><p>Collect around a documented security or fraud-prevention action, not continuously because the SDK is available.</p></div><div class="decision-item"><h3>Select probe groups</h3><p>Enable only the categories needed to answer the stated question. Prefer coarse observations over high-entropy fields.</p></div><div class="decision-item"><h3>Project fields</h3><p>Remove unused values before the event reaches your backend. Avoid storing raw location or local addresses when a coarser derived feature is sufficient.</p></div><div class="decision-item"><h3>Expire data</h3><p>Set a retention period tied to investigation, model training, legal, and audit needs. Delete raw values earlier than derived aggregates when possible.</p></div></div></section>
      <section id="govern"><h2>Production governance checklist</h2><ul><li>Document purpose and data owner for each enabled probe.</li><li>Map platform and regional disclosure or consent requirements.</li><li>Restrict access to raw observations and record administrative access.</li><li>Encrypt events in transit and at rest using the host system controls.</li><li>Version backend feature logic and monitor schema drift.</li><li>Support deletion, retention enforcement, and incident response.</li><li>Review false-positive impact across device age, region, platform, and accessibility use.</li></ul></section>
      <section id="sensitive"><h2>High-sensitivity areas</h2><table><thead><tr><th>Area</th><th>Why it needs review</th><th>Safer pattern</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Location</td><td>Can reveal precise movement or home and work patterns.</td><td>Use existing permission state or coarse server-derived consistency where adequate.</td></tr><tr><td>Telephony</td><td>Carrier, SIM, and country can expose personal context.</td><td>Store only the comparison result when raw values are unnecessary.</td></tr><tr><td>Accessibility</td><td>May relate to disability and legitimate assistive technology.</td><td>Never treat accessibility use itself as fraud.</td></tr><tr><td>Screen and touch observation</td><td>Screenshot detection is user-visible and overlay flags can have legitimate causes.</td><td>Keep transaction safety off by default and observe only protected flows. Declare <code>android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_CAPTURE</code> or <code>android.permission.DETECT_SCREEN_RECORDING</code> in the host app only when the corresponding field is required.</td></tr><tr><td>Local addresses</td><td>High entropy and network-sensitive.</td><td>Prefer a boolean or coarse network class if it serves the purpose.</td></tr><tr><td>Known apps</td><td>App visibility can expose behavior or affiliations.</td><td>Use the finite audited list, document each entry, and never request broad package visibility.</td></tr></tbody></table></section>
      <section id="decisions"><h2>Responsible decision use</h2><div class="notice warning">Never block or decline a person based on one signal. Provide recovery or review paths for consequential outcomes.</div><p>Root, jailbreak, emulator, VPN, accessibility, screen capture, and remote-access observations all have legitimate explanations. Combine calibrated mobile evidence with authenticated account, transaction, behavioral, and server-side context. Monitor false positives and distribution shifts after every rule or model change.</p><p>This documentation is engineering guidance, not legal advice. Involve privacy, legal, security, and risk owners for your product and jurisdictions.</p></section>
    </article>
  </main>
  <footer class="site-footer"><div class="container footer-wrap"><div>Purpose first. Collection second.</div><div class="footer-links"><a href="../signals/">Signal catalog</a><a href="../risk-teams/">Risk guide</a><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/security">Security</a></div></div></footer>
</body>
</html>

```

`website/risk-teams/index.html`:

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>Risk Team Playbook | Mobile Device Signals</title>
  <meta name="description" content="A practical playbook for risk and fraud teams using mobile device observations for feature engineering, calibration, scoring models, investigations, and monitoring.">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/risk-teams/">
  <meta property="og:title" content="Mobile Device Signals for Risk Teams"><meta property="og:description" content="From raw observations to governed, explainable backend features."><meta property="og:type" content="article"><meta property="og:url" content="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/risk-teams/">
  <link rel="icon" href="../assets/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml"><link rel="stylesheet" href="../assets/styles.css"><script defer src="../assets/site.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <a class="skip-link" href="#content">Skip to content</a>
  <header class="site-header"><div class="container nav-wrap"><a class="brand" href="../"><span class="brand-mark">RS</span><span>Device Risk Signals</span></a><button class="menu-button" type="button" data-menu-button aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="site-navigation">Menu</button><nav class="site-nav" id="site-navigation" data-navigation aria-label="Primary navigation"><a href="../signals/">Signals</a><a href="../integration/">Integration</a><a href="../privacy/">Privacy</a><a href="./" aria-current="page">Risk teams</a><a href="../faq/">FAQ</a><a class="nav-github" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">GitHub</a></nav></div></header>
  <main id="content" class="container docs-layout">
    <aside class="sidebar"><strong>On this page</strong><a href="#role">SDK role</a><a href="#pipeline">Feature pipeline</a><a href="#families">Feature families</a><a href="#example">Worked example</a><a href="#calibration">Calibration</a><a href="#guardrails">Guardrails</a></aside>
    <article class="prose">
      <header class="page-intro"><p class="eyebrow">Risk and fraud operations</p><h1>Use device context without hiding the reasoning</h1><p class="lede">Turn mobile observations into versioned backend features that can be calibrated, explained, monitored, and challenged.</p></header>
      <section id="role"><h2>The SDK is an evidence source</h2><p>The SDK does not calculate a risk score, return a trusted verdict, or decide whether an action should proceed. It produces client-side observations that your trusted backend may use alongside account, transaction, behavioral, and platform-attestation evidence.</p><div class="notice warning">Never block or decline a customer based on one signal. A single observation can be stale, unavailable, attacker-controlled, or legitimate in context.</div></section>
      <section id="pipeline"><h2>A defensible feature pipeline</h2><div class="pipeline" aria-label="Feature engineering pipeline"><div>Raw event</div><div>Quality checks</div><div>Derived features</div><div>Model or policy</div><div>Outcome monitoring</div></div><ol><li><strong>Bind the event.</strong> Associate collection with an authenticated session and a server-known action identifier.</li><li><strong>Validate quality.</strong> Check schema, SDK version, timestamp, probe outcome, platform, and freshness.</li><li><strong>Derive on the backend.</strong> Convert raw values into stable, documented comparisons or categories.</li><li><strong>Calibrate with labels.</strong> Estimate lift, coverage, false-positive impact, and interactions using representative historical outcomes.</li><li><strong>Monitor after release.</strong> Track drift, missingness, latency, outcome disparity, appeals, and rule contribution.</li></ol></section>
      <section id="families"><h2>Feature families and cautious interpretations</h2><table><thead><tr><th>Family</th><th>Example derived feature</th><th>What it may support</th><th>Common caveat</th></tr></thead><tbody>
        <tr><td>Application provenance</td><td><code>signing_certificate_expected</code>, <code>installer_known</code>, <code>debuggable_release</code></td><td>Repackaging and unofficial distribution investigation</td><td>Enterprise, QA, and alternative-store delivery can be legitimate</td></tr>
        <tr><td>OS integrity</td><td><code>integrity_evidence_count</code>, grouped by evidence family</td><td>Instrumentation or modified-environment context</td><td>Client checks are bypassable and device-specific</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Device security posture</td><td><code>secure_lock_available</code>, <code>trusted_time_disabled</code>, <code>patch_age_bucket</code></td><td>Step-up eligibility and investigation context</td><td>Capability differs by hardware and OS; patch age needs external mapping</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Transaction safety</td><td><code>capture_active_at_action</code>, <code>remote_access_observed</code></td><td>Point-in-time review or user warning flows</td><td>Accessibility and support sessions are legitimate</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Consistency</td><td><code>sim_network_country_mismatch</code>, <code>locale_timezone_mismatch</code>, <code>app_runtime_platform_mismatch</code></td><td>Contradictory context that merits corroboration</td><td>Travel, roaming, multilingual users, and stale location are common</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Collection health</td><td><code>probe_timeout_rate</code>, <code>required_probe_missing</code></td><td>Telemetry quality and tampering investigation</td><td>Older devices and OS changes can affect probe reliability</td></tr>
      </tbody></table><p>Velocity, account graph, IP reputation, transaction amount, behavioral sequence, and prior outcomes belong to the backend. They are not device SDK responsibilities.</p></section>
      <section id="example"><h2>Worked example: account recovery</h2><p>Assume the customer is recovering an account from a mobile device. A backend feature view might contain:</p><div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" type="button" data-copy="risk-example">Copy</button><pre id="risk-example"><code>{
  "action": "account_recovery",
  "telemetry_quality": {
    "sdk_major": 0,
    "event_age_bucket": "under_30s",
    "required_probes_complete": true
  },
  "application_provenance": {
    "bundle_expected": true,
    "signing_certificate_expected": true,
    "debuggable_release": false
  },
  "integrity": {
    "emulator_observed": false,
    "debugger_observed": false,
    "strong_integrity_evidence_count": 0
  },
  "consistency": {
    "sim_network_country_mismatch": false,
    "locale_timezone_mismatch": null
  },
  "transaction_context": {
    "screen_capture_observed": false,
    "remote_access_observed": null
  }
}</code></pre></div><p><code>null</code> preserves unavailable or inapplicable evidence. It must not be silently converted to <code>false</code>. The feature view may contribute to a model or policy, but the decision should also consider authenticated account history, recovery method strength, transaction context, and a customer recovery path.</p><h3>Consistency helpers</h3><p>The package includes <code>deriveConsistencySignals()</code> for deterministic comparisons and <code>deriveObservationMetrics()</code> for bounded ratios, counts, and consistency checks over already-collected values. They omit unavailable calculations and never add weights, an identifier, or a verdict.</p></section>
      <section id="calibration"><h2>Calibration before enforcement</h2><div class="decision-list"><div class="decision-item"><h3>Shadow mode</h3><p>Collect features without affecting customer outcomes. Establish coverage and operational stability across supported platforms.</p></div><div class="decision-item"><h3>Labeled analysis</h3><p>Measure conditional lift against confirmed outcomes. Segment by app version, OS, hardware class, geography, and accessibility context.</p></div><div class="decision-item"><h3>Threshold review</h3><p>Choose action thresholds from observed tradeoffs, not intuition. Separate allow, step-up, manual review, and decline objectives.</p></div><div class="decision-item"><h3>Ongoing monitoring</h3><p>Watch population stability, missingness, feature contribution, false positives, complaints, and recovery success after deployment.</p></div></div></section>
      <section id="guardrails"><h2>Operational guardrails</h2><ul><li>Keep raw and derived schemas versioned and auditable.</li><li>Do not train on a proxy label created by the same rule you are evaluating.</li><li>Prevent future-outcome leakage when building historical datasets.</li><li>Use time-based validation for changing fraud patterns.</li><li>Review accessibility, travel, developer, refurbished-device, and low-cost-device cohorts.</li><li>Require corroboration for high-impact actions and preserve reason codes.</li><li>Maintain a safe recovery or human-review path.</li><li>Recalibrate after SDK, OS, app-signing, or probe implementation changes.</li></ul><div class="notice">The useful unit is not “risky device.” It is a well-defined observation, collected for a specific action, interpreted with evidence from the rest of the system.</div></section>
    </article>
  </main>
  <footer class="site-footer"><div class="container footer-wrap"><div>Explainable observations for governed risk systems.</div><div class="footer-links"><a href="../signals/">Signal catalog</a><a href="../privacy/">Privacy</a><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/discussions">Discuss</a></div></div></footer>
</body>
</html>

```

`website/signals/index.html`:

```html
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8"><meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>Device Signal Catalog | React Native Risk Signals</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Design a backend contract from the complete Android and iOS device signal table, including every public probe, selectable field, outcome, and platform boundary.">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/signals/">
  <meta property="og:title" content="React Native Device Signal and Backend Contract Catalog"><meta property="og:description" content="Every public probe and selectable field with platforms, outcomes, permissions, sensitivity, defaults, and missing-data semantics."><meta property="og:type" content="article"><meta property="og:url" content="https://afanasievn.github.io/react-native-device-risk-signals/signals/">
  <link rel="icon" href="../assets/favicon.svg" type="image/svg+xml"><link rel="stylesheet" href="../assets/styles.css"><script defer src="../assets/site.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
  <a class="skip-link" href="#content">Skip to content</a>
  <header class="site-header"><div class="container nav-wrap"><a class="brand" href="../"><span class="brand-mark">RS</span><span>Device Risk Signals</span></a><button class="menu-button" type="button" data-menu-button aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="site-navigation">Menu</button><nav class="site-nav" id="site-navigation" data-navigation aria-label="Primary navigation"><a href="./" aria-current="page">Signals</a><a href="../integration/">Integration</a><a href="../privacy/">Privacy</a><a href="../risk-teams/">Risk teams</a><a href="../faq/">FAQ</a><a class="nav-github" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals">GitHub</a></nav></div></header>
  <main id="content" class="container docs-layout">
    <aside class="sidebar"><strong>On this page</strong><a href="#model">Collection model</a><a href="#contract">Backend contract</a><a href="#catalog">Complete probe table</a><a href="#outcomes">Probe outcomes</a><a href="#source">Source of truth</a></aside>
    <article class="prose">
      <header class="page-intro"><p class="eyebrow">Machine-readable inventory</p><h1>Device signal and backend contract catalog</h1><p class="lede">Every public probe and selectable top-level field, with platform, sensitivity, permission, default-state, and missing-data semantics.</p><div class="button-row"><a class="button" href="../probe-catalog.json">Download probe-catalog.json</a><a class="button secondary" href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/blob/main/src/NativeDeviceIntel.ts">View exact TypeScript field types</a></div></header>
      <section id="model"><h2>How collection is organized</h2><p>A probe is an independently executed group of related observations. Each probe can succeed, be skipped, time out, or return an error without invalidating the rest of the event.</p><div class="notice">Field availability depends on the platform, OS version, device hardware, host-app permissions, and collection configuration. Missing does not mean safe.</div></section>
      <section id="contract">
        <h2>Backend event contract</h2>
        <p>Design the ingestion boundary around the stable event envelope and an open map of probe outcomes. Probe IDs and successful data fields are additive, so consumers should tolerate unknown keys while validating the fields they use.</p>
        <div class="code-block"><button class="copy-button" type="button" data-copy="backend-contract">Copy</button><pre id="backend-contract"><code>type RawSignalEvent = {
  session_id: string;
  client_id?: string;
  event_type: "device_intel_collection";
  schema_version: number;
  collected_at: string; // ISO 8601
  probes: Record&lt;string,
    | { status: "success"; data: Record&lt;string, unknown&gt; }
    | { status: "skipped"; reason: string }
    | { status: "timeout" }
    | { status: "error"; error: string }
  &gt;;
};</code></pre></div>
        <div class="contract-rules" aria-label="Backend contract rules">
          <div><strong>Version the envelope</strong><p><code>schema_version</code> changes only when the event envelope changes. New probe IDs can appear without an envelope version bump.</p></div>
          <div><strong>Keep absence distinct</strong><p>An omitted field, a skipped probe, a timeout, an error, and an observed <code>false</code> are different states.</p></div>
          <div><strong>Allow additive data</strong><p>Persist the SDK version with your event and ignore unknown probe IDs or fields until your backend explicitly adopts them.</p></div>
          <div><strong>Validate successful data</strong><p>Use the table below for selectable names and the exported TypeScript types for exact scalar, array, and nested-object shapes.</p></div>
        </div>
      </section>
      <section id="catalog">
        <h2>Probe catalog</h2><p>Use the filters to review platform coverage. High-sensitivity and expensive probes require extra purpose, retention, and calibration review.</p>
        <div class="catalog-controls" role="group" aria-label="Filter signals by platform"><button class="filter-button" type="button" data-filter="all" aria-pressed="true">All</button><button class="filter-button" type="button" data-filter="android" aria-pressed="false">Android</button><button class="filter-button" type="button" data-filter="ios" aria-pressed="false">iOS</button></div>
        <div class="catalog-table-wrap" tabindex="0" aria-label="Complete public probe catalog">
          <table class="probe-contract-table">
            <thead><tr><th>Probe and purpose</th><th>Availability</th><th>Data classification</th><th>Permission behavior and notes</th><th>Possible success data</th></tr></thead>
            <tbody>
          <!-- GENERATED_PROBE_ROWS_START -->
          <tr data-probe-id="device_identity" data-platform="android ios">
            <td class="probe-summary"><code>device_identity</code><strong>Device identity</strong><p>Describe the claimed device and operating-system build for consistency checks.</p></td>
            <td><div class="table-tags"><span class="tag">Android + iOS</span><span class="tag">default on</span></div></td>
            <td><div class="table-tags"><span class="tag high">high sensitivity</span><span class="tag">device</span><span class="tag">operating_system</span></div></td>
            <td><p class="permission-copy">No permission required</p></td>
            <td><details class="field-disclosure"><summary>13 top-level fields</summary><div class="field-list"><code>manufacturer</code><code>model</code><code>brand</code><code>systemName</code><code>systemVersion</code><code>isTablet</code><code>osBuild</code><code>kernelVersion</code><code>kernelOsRelease</code><code>kernelOsType</code><code>androidBuild</code><code>isIosAppOnMac</code><code>isMacCatalystApp</code></div></details></td>
          </tr>
          <tr data-probe-id="hardware" data-platform="android ios">
            <td class="probe-summary"><code>hardware</code><strong>Hardware</strong><p>Collect coarse hardware, display, memory, battery, and device-class observations.</p></td>
            <td><div class="table-tags"><span class="tag">Android + iOS</span><span class="tag">default on</span></div></td>
            <td><div class="table-tags"><span class="tag high">high sensitivity</span><span class="tag">hardware</span><span class="tag">display</span><span class="tag">battery</span><span class="tag">storage</span><span class="tag">nfc_state</span></div></td>
            <td><p class="permission-copy">No permission required</p><p class="probe-notes">Detailed battery/power and NFC state are Android-only. Storage and uptime are Android-only because the corresponding iOS APIs require additional declarations.</p></td>
            <td><details class="field-disclosure"><summary>35 top-level fields</summary><div class="field-list"><code>screenWidthPx</code><code>screenHeightPx</code><code>screenDensity</code><code>screenDpi</code><code>screenPhysicalWidthPx</code><code>screenPhysicalHeightPx</code><code>screenPhysicalDensity</code><code>screenBrightness</code><code>screenOrientation</code><code>processorCount</code><code>cpuArchitecture</code><code>cpuMaxFrequencyMhz</code><code>totalMemoryBytes</code><code>freeMemoryBytes</code><code>isLowMemory</code><code>processResidentMemoryBytes</code><code>isLowRamDevice</code><code>runtimeMaxMemoryBytes</code><code>lowPowerModeEnabled</code><code>batteryLevel</code><code>batteryState</code><code>batteryTemperatureC</code><code>batteryHealth</code><code>batteryVoltageMv</code><code>batteryTechnology</code><code>batteryPresent</code><code>batteryLow</code><code>powerSource</code><code>batteryCycleCount</code><code>chargeTimeRemainingMs</code><code>nfcAvailable</code><code>nfcEnabled</code><code>storageTotalBytes</code><code>storageFreeBytes</code><code>uptimeMs</code></div></details></td>
          </tr>
          <tr data-probe-id="fonts" data-platform="android ios">
            <td class="probe-summary"><code>fonts</code><strong>Installed fonts</strong><p>Produce a platform-local digest of installed font names for consistency analysis.</p></td>

      </section>
      <section id="outcomes"><h2>Independent probe outcomes</h2><table><thead><tr><th>Outcome</th><th>Meaning</th><th>Backend treatment</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><code>success</code></td><td>The probe returned an observation payload.</td><td>Validate schema and freshness before feature use.</td></tr><tr><td><code>skipped</code></td><td>Configuration, consent, platform, or capability excluded the probe.</td><td>Keep separate from negative evidence.</td></tr><tr><td><code>timeout</code></td><td>The configured deadline elapsed.</td><td>Monitor collection health, do not convert to a safe value.</td></tr><tr><td><code>error</code></td><td>The probe failed in isolation.</td><td>Track error class and SDK version operationally.</td></tr></tbody></table></section>
      <section id="source"><h2>Source of truth</h2><p>The typed catalog in <code>src/probeCatalog.ts</code> is authoritative. It includes every selectable field, purpose, data category, platform, sensitivity level, permission note, and default state.</p><p><a href="https://github.com/AfanasievN/react-native-device-risk-signals/blob/main/docs/DATA_DICTIONARY.md">Open the full data dictionary on GitHub</a>.</p></section>
```
